prompt: Which one of the following is true about color map 1?
choices: Red indicates flow toward the transducer.
Red indicates flow away from the transducer.
Blue indicates laminar flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which one of the following is true about color map 1?
choices: Blue indicates flow toward the transducer.
Red indicates flow away from the transducer.
Blue indicates laminar flow away from the transducer.
Blue indicates flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which of the following is true about color map 2?
choices: Blue indicates flow toward the transducer.
Blue indicates flow away from the transducer.
Dark blue indicates laminar flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which of the following is true about color map 2?
choices: Green indicates higher velocity flow toward the transducer.
Red indicates flow away from the transducer.
Green indicates higher volume flow toward the transducer.
Blue indicates laminar flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which statement is true about color map 1?
choices: Red and yellow indicate flow toward the transducer.
Red indicates flow away from the transducer.
Green indicates laminar flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which statement is true about color map 1?
choices: Red and yellow indicate flow away from the transducer.
Red indicates flow away from the transducer.
Green indicates turbulent flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which statement is the best reason why color map 1 is a variance mode map?
choices: Red indicates flow toward the transducer.
Blue indicates flow away from the transducer.
Blue and green indicate flow away from the transducer.
Yellow indicates flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which statement is true about color map 2?
choices: Light blue indicates flow toward the transducer.
Red indicates flow away from the transducer.
Dark blue indicates laminar flow away from the transducer.

prompt: Which statement is true about color map 2?
choices: Red indicates turbulent flow toward the transducer.
Red indicates flow away from the transducer.
Light blue indicates laminar flow toward the transducer.

prompt: In Fig. 19.34, in which direction does blood flow through the top vessel?
choices: left to right, then right to left
right to left, then left to right

prompt: In Fig. 19.34, what does the black area (yellow arrow) in the center of both vessels represent?
In Fig. 19.35, in which direction is blood flowing through the vessel?
choices: left to right, then right to left
right to left, then left to right

prompt: The Doppler effect is presented as a _____ when the source and the receiver are _____.
choices: Frequency shift, in motion relative to each other.
Frequency increase, moving together at the same speed.

prompt: Starting from the same point, the sound source is moving toward the east at 12 miles/hour and the receiver is moving toward the west at 10 miles/hour. The Doppler shift is ____ [+ or -].
Starting from the same point, the receiver is moving toward the west at 12 miles/hour and the source is moving toward the west at 10 miles/hour. The Doppler shift is ____ [+ or -].
Doppler shift produces information about _____.
At what angle between the sound beam and the direction of motion will the Doppler shift be the highest?
At what angle between the sound beam and the direction of motion will the Doppler shift be closest to zero?
What is the difference between speed and velocity?
choices: Speed has only magnitude; velocity has magnitude and direction.
Velocity has only magnitude; speed has magnitude and direction.
Speed is always greater than velocity.

prompt: What is the current method of processing Doppler signals?
choices: Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) for pulsed and CW spectral analysis, and autocorrelation for color flow Doppler.
